bugs.error_message: TailState's head-hash rotation check flagged every append as a rotation when the file was shorter than HEAD_HASH_BYTES
bugs.root_cause: The hash was computed over min(HEAD_HASH_BYTES, current_size) on every poll. Growing the file from N to N+M bytes meant the next poll hashed N+M bytes -- a strictly longer prefix -- so the hash naturally differed and looked like a rotation.
bugs.fix: Pin head_prefix_len at construction (or after a reset_to/rotation) and rehash EXACTLY that prefix length on every subsequent poll. Growth keeps the anchored bytes unchanged so the hash is stable; only a rewrite flips it. Below-prefix-length shrinks are caught by the earlier size-shrink branch.

bugs.error_message: E0117 (orphan rule) when adding impl RecordScanner for Box<dyn RecordScanner + Send + Sync> in clog-app
bugs.root_cause: Both the trait and the Box type live outside clog-app, so the impl is orphan-forbidden. The motivation was to let the tail task hold a runtime-selected scanner across polls without caring whether it was a PatternLayout or a regex.
bugs.fix: Introduced a sized sum type (CompiledScanner { Pattern(CompiledPattern) | Regex(RegexScanner) }) that owns the impl in clog-app. Store ScannerKind (source strings) on OpenedFile and call ScannerKind::compile() each poll to materialise a CompiledScanner. Recompilation is cheap relative to disk I/O.

bugs.error_message: User reported: when detached (follow-tail off), incoming tail appends caused the viewport to visually shift/scroll, so users couldn't stably read a chosen section while tailing remained active.
bugs.root_cause: Two contributing causes. (1) On every Appended delta the UI was calling pages.value.delete(lastPage) followed by an implicit re-fetch via the virtualRows watcher. The delete blanked the cached page synchronously, so visible rows on that page rendered as empty placeholders until the next IPC round trip finished -- visually reading as a shift. (2) No defensive re-anchor of scrollTop across the line_count bump, so any internal virtualizer measurement that happened to nudge scrollTop would not be corrected.
bugs.fix: Replaced delete-then-refetch with an in-place force-refetch (fetchPage gained a `force` flag; cached entries stay rendered until the new payload arrives, then swap atomically via pages.value.set + new Map()). For the detached case, capture scrollTop before the count bump and re-assert it across two requestAnimationFrame ticks (one for the synchronous count change, one for any deferred virtualizer measurement). Follow-tail path still calls jumpToBottom() as before.

bugs.error_message: proptest 'prod_roundtrips' began failing 'must parse as header' on every input after extending BUILTIN_PATTERNS with new entries.
bugs.root_cause: The proptest indexed BUILTIN_PATTERNS by position (BUILTIN_PATTERNS[1].1) to look up the prod pattern. When new patterns were inserted, index 1 no longer pointed at prod, so the test compiled the wrong pattern and rejected every generated prod-shaped line. Same vulnerability existed in the unit tests for `parse_prod_header_no_logger` and `parse_log4j2_default_header`.
bugs.fix: Added a `builtin_pattern(name: &str) -> Option<&'static str>` lookup helper to clog-core::pattern (re-exported from lib.rs) and switched every test (unit + proptest) from BUILTIN_PATTERNS[i] indexing to builtin_pattern("name").unwrap(). The position-stable lookup makes future reordering safe.
